title: Overview of the EAM Framework
text: modular simulation engine built upon generalized evidence accumulation framework, within assume decision making characterized stochastic process evidence continuously collected time. accumulation unfolds infinitesimal increments dtdt, can approximated practice small time steps (example, 1ms}). moment--moment change accumulated evidence x(t)x(t) can expressed stochastic differential: dx(t)=vŌŗģdt+sŌŗģŽā, dx(t) = v \cdot dt + s \cdot \sigma, vv drift rate, dtdt time step, ss noise scaling parameter, Žā\sigma diffusion noise term. process unfolds, accumulated evidence x(t)x(t) continues evolve reaches decision boundaries, point response triggered corresponding RT recorded. distance reaching boundary jointly determined two parameters: decision boundary (aa) starting point (zz). evidence accumulates, process continues x(t)+zx(t) + z first reaches either upper boundary aa falls lower boundary 0 (two-boundary EAMs). elapsed time start accumulation boundary crossing defines decision time; adding non-decision time t0t_{0} yields observable RT. modular simulation engine allows researchers flexibly simulate different types data changing evidence accumulation algorithm, number accumulators involved process, decision termination rules. engine composed ten modules (see figure ): Number accumulators: number accumulators race compete toward decision boundary (e.g., one DDM LFM; multiple LBA, LCA, RDM) Prior distribution hyperparameters: hierarchical priors specifying mean, variance, regression coefficients core parameters evidence-accumulation process including drift rate, decision boundary, starting point/relative bias, non-decision time, leakage parameter, strength lateral inhibition, stability parameter. -trial variability: specify formulas -subject -trial variability parameters, also allowing linking covariates subject- trial-level parameters. Item-level formula: linking accumulatorŌĆÖs parameter item indexes output positions generate accumulator-level parameter values drift rate decision boundary, also allowing linking covariates item-level parameters. Type noise: family diffusion noise (e.g., Gaussian vs.┬ĀL├®vy ╬▒\alpha-stable vs.┬Ādeterministic ballistic). Simulation setting: configuration used model simulation, including model equations, number conditions, number trials per condition, implementation parameters. Rules record responses: criteria determining responses recorded (e.g., first boundary crossing , first nn releases within time limit). Time step: discrete increment used approximate continuous-time evidence accumulation (default 1,). Noise setting: algorithmic specification stochastic increments (e.g., additive vs.┬Āmultiplicative). Type model: underlying architecture evidence accumulation (e.g., single-boundary vs.┬Ādouble-boundary vs.┬ĀLCA accumulators). combining different modular components, engine can simulate responses RTs many representative EAMs (see section Models eam configurations representative EAM). data simulated, can directly passed inference module parameter estimation.

what: A standard workflow in the eam package
title: Overview of the EAM Framework
text: standard procedure simulation-based inference involves several steps: First, generative model simulator M(╬Ė)M(\theta) defined, specifies observable data yy generated underlying parameters ╬Ė\theta. Second, parameters sampled prior distribution ŽĆ(╬Ė)\pi(\theta), reflecting prior beliefs theoretical constraints. Third, sampled parameter value, synthetic datasets ysimy_{\text{sim}} simulated model, producing pairs parameters corresponding simulated observations. Fourth, similarity simulated observed data quantifiedŌĆöeither computing distance metrics summary statistics, Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC) Amortized Bayesian Inference (ABI). Finally, approximate posterior distribution p(╬ĖŌłŻyobs)p(\theta \mid y_{\text{obs}}) obtained weighting sampling parameters according similarity likelihood estimate. following sections provide structured overview standard simulation inference workflow supported package. Section 1: Getting Started: Minimal Working Example introduces core functions package provides minimal runnable example based three-parameter Drift Diffusion Model (DDM). Section 2: Models eam demonstrates representative evidence accumulation models can specified within package, can extended multi-response settings intergeted covariates. Section 3: Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference Free Recall presents real-world application using free recall data, illustrating package can applied empirical datasets practice. Reference: Beaumont, M. ., Zhang, W., & Balding, D. J. (2002). Approximate Bayesian computation population genetics. Genetics, 162 (4), 2025ŌĆō2035. https://doi.org/10.1093/genetics/162.4.2025 Blum, M. G. B., & Fran├¦ois, O. (2010). Non-linear regression models approximate Bayesian computation. Statistics Computing, 20 (1), 63ŌĆō73. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11222-009-9116-0 Csill├®ry, K., Fran├¦ois, O., & Blum, M. G. B. (2012). abc: R package approximate Bayesian computation (ABC). Methods Ecology Evolution, 3 (3), 475ŌĆō479. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.2041-210x.2011.00179.x Sainsbury-Dale, M., Zammit-Mangion, ., & Huser, R. (2024). title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: details model listed : Priors global parameters A0Ōł╝Uniform(1,5),V0Ōł╝Uniform(0.5,3),ndt0Ōł╝Uniform(0,1),Žü0=0.5. \begin{aligned} A_0 &\sim \text{Uniform}(1, 5), \\ V_0 &\sim \text{Uniform}(0.5, 3), \\ ndt_0 &\sim \text{Uniform}(0, 1), \\ \rho_0 &= 0.5 . \end{aligned} Item-level parameterization Žü=Žü0,Aupper=ŽüA0,Alower=ŌłÆŽüA0,V=V0,ndt=ndt0. \begin{aligned} \rho &= \rho_0, \\ A_{\text{upper}} &= \rho A_0, \\ A_{\text{lower}} &= -\rho A_0, \\ V &= V_0, \\ ndt &= ndt_0 . \end{aligned} Evidence accumulation dynamics dX(t)=Vdt+dW(t),dW(t)Ōł╝­ØÆ®(0,dt). dX(t) = V \, dt + dW(t), \qquad dW(t) \sim \mathcal{N}(0, dt). decision made X(t)X(t) first reaches either AupperA_{\text{upper}} AlowerA_{\text{lower}}, observed response time RT=Tdecision+ndt. RT = T_{\text{decision}} + ndt .

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: First, load required packages. , specify model configuration according setup described .
code: # Load necessary packages library(eam) library(dplyr) #> Warning: ń©ŗÕ║ÅÕīģ'dplyr'µś»ńö©Rńēłµ£¼4.5.2 µØźÕ╗║ķĆĀńÜä library(rtdists) library(tidyr) ####################### # Model specification # ####################### # Define the number of accumulators n_items <- 1  # Set the number of accumulators in the model prior_params <- tibble(   n_items = n_items )  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(1, 5),   # Drift rate   V_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Relative starting point   rho_0 ~ 0.5 )  # Specify the between-trial components (skipped here) between_trial_formulas <- list()  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Relative starting point   rho ~ rho_0,   # Decision boundary   A_upper ~ rho * (A_0), # upper   A_lower ~ -rho * (A_0), # lower   # Drift rate   V ~ V_0,   # Non-decision time   ndt ~ ndt_0 )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: model specified, can proceed data simulation. simulation step generates large collection synthetic datasets sampling parameters prior distributions simulating data corresponding generative model. simulated dataset represents possible outcome implied assumed model. example simulated dataset. row dataset represents single simulated decision trial. columns defined follows: condition_idx: Index experimental condition trial simulated. trial_idx: Index trial within condition. rank_idx: Rank position associated item response simulated task (e.g., output order recall decision sequence). item_idx: Index item evaluated responded trial. rt: Simulated response time decision trial. choice: Binary decision outcome trial (e.g., 1 = correct / upper option; 0 = incorrect / lower option). model parameters (A_0, V_0, ndt_0, rho_0): Parameters underlying decision model used generate data chunk_idx: Index identifying simulated batch chunk data generation, useful tracking parallel segmented simulation runs.
code: #################### # Simulation setup # #################### sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 5000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "ddm",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE, # In tutorial, we disable parallelization, but you are encouraged to enable it locally   n_cores = NULL, # When parallel enabled, will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  ################## # Run simulation # ################## sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config ) sim_output_data <- sim_output$open_dataset() |> dplyr::collect()  head(sim_output_data) #>   condition_idx trial_idx rank_idx item_idx        rt choice      A_0      V_0     ndt_0 rho_0 #> 1             1         1        1        1 0.4260942      1 1.975088 2.109242 0.2750942   0.5 #> 2             1         2        1        1 0.7960942      1 1.975088 2.109242 0.2750942   0.5 #> 3             1         3        1        1 0.4510942      1 1.975088 2.109242 0.2750942   0.5 #> 4             1         4        1        1 0.5060942      1 1.975088 2.109242 0.2750942   0.5 #> 5             1         5        1        1 0.4760942      1 1.975088 2.109242 0.2750942   0.5 #> 6             1         6        1        1 0.5180942     -1 1.975088 2.109242 0.2750942   0.5 #>   chunk_idx #> 1         1 #> 2         1 #> 3         1 #> 4         1 #> 5         1 #> 6         1

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: observed data, create small dataset using rtdists package, provides fast simulators standard DDM. Specifically, simulate N = 500 trials two-boundary DDM fixed parameters (aa, vv, t0t0) aa controls boundary separation, vv drift rate, t0t0 captures non-decision time. function rdiffusion() returns response labels ("upper"/"lower") response times. consistency data format produced simulation engine, recode responses numeric choice variable (1 upper boundary -1 lower boundary), add condition_idx field indicate trials belong single experimental condition. Finally, observed dataset organized follows:
code: ############################ # Observed data generation # ############################  N <- 1000  pars <- list(   a  = 2.0,   # Decision boundary   v  = 1.0,   # Drift rate   t0 = 0.5  # Non-decision time )  # rdiffusion() will return responses and RTs from a standard DDM observed_data <- rdiffusion(n = N, a = pars$a, v = pars$v, t0 = pars$t0)  # Recode the data to be consistent with simulated data observed_data$choice <- ifelse(observed_data$response == "upper", 1, -1) observed_data$condition_idx <- 1 head(observed_data) #>          rt response choice condition_idx #> 1 1.2231327    upper      1             1 #> 2 1.0655246    upper      1             1 #> 3 1.0629394    upper      1             1 #> 4 0.8827105    upper      1             1 #> 5 1.4909006    upper      1             1 #> 6 0.8059849    upper      1             1

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: define summary pipeline extracts two types common summary statistics DDM data: () accuracy (ii) RT quantiles (10%, 30%, 50%, 70%, 90%) computed separately choice category (1 vs.┬Ā-1). apply summary procedure every simulated dataset obtain simulation_sumstat, observed dataset obtain target_sumstat. Finally, build_abc_input() aligns simulated observed summaries consistent structure.
code: ##################### # abc model prepare # #####################  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(choice == 1) / sum(!is.na(choice)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "choice"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE, # turn on if you want to speed up   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )  # Calculate observed summary statistics target_sumstat <- summary_pipe(observed_data)  # Align the simulated and observed summary statistics abc_input <- build_abc_input(   simulation_output = sim_output,   simulation_summary = simulation_sumstat,   target_summary     = target_sumstat,   param = c("A_0", "V_0", "ndt_0") )

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: simulated observed summary statistics aligned, can now estimate posterior distribution model parameters using Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC). Specifically, fit ABC model comparing simulated summary statistics observed summary statistics. retain (reweight) parameter draws best reproduce observed summaries tolerance level (tol = 0.05). use regression-based-adjusted ABC variant (method = "loclinear"), learns flexible mapping summary statistics parameters improve posterior approximation. diagnostics help verify whether posterior concentrates plausible regions parameter space whether recovered parameters consistent parameters rdiffusion() model. fitting, summarize posterior means/medians 95% credible intervals visualize resulting posterior distributions. shown, true parameter values underlying observed data lie well within 95% credible intervals posterior distributions.
code: ######################## # Parameter estimation # ########################  # Fit abc model (neuralnet) abc_fit <- abc::abc(   target = abc_input$target,   param  = abc_input$param,   sumstat= abc_input$sumstat,   tol    = 0.05,   method = "neuralnet" ) #> Warning: All parameters are "none" transformed. # Posterior estimation check summarise_posterior_parameters(   abc_fit,   ci_level = 0.95 ) #>   parameter      mean    median ci_lower_0.025 ci_upper_0.975 #> 1       A_0 2.1521676 2.1329682      1.8822137      2.4647523 #> 2       V_0 0.9994105 0.9967066      0.7438985      1.2538704 #> 3     ndt_0 0.4429218 0.4499385      0.3326319      0.5312672  plot_posterior_parameters(   abc_fit,   abc_input )

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: next step evaluate parameter recovery model. , run cv4abc() cross-validation procedure. First, specify number validation datasets (N = 100). function randomly samples N conditions simulated summary statistics, treats associated parameter values known ground truth, re-estimates parameters using fitted ABC object tolerance level. Recovery performance visualized using plot_cv_recovery(), compares estimated parameters true values across validation datasets. Good recovery indicated high correlation estimated true parameters (good: rŌēź0.75r \ge 0.75; excellent: rŌēź0.90r \ge 0.90) estimates clustering closely around identity line minimal residual dispersion. resid_tol option trims extreme residuals (retaining central 99%), making main recovery pattern easier inspect. shown, three parameters exhibit excellent recovery. final diagnostic, perform posterior predictive check assess adequacy fitted model. Specifically, generate posterior data using posterior median estimates obtained ABC analysis. resulting posterior predictive simulations compared observed dataset. visually inspecting overlap simulated observed RT distributions accuracy rates, can evaluate whether fitted model able reproduce key empirical patterns.
code: # Parameter recovery check abc_cv <- abc_cv(   abc_input = abc_input,   abc_result = abc_fit,   nval = 100,   tols = c(0.05) ) plot_cv_recovery(   abc_cv,   n_rows = 3,   n_cols = 1,   resid_tol = 0.99,   interactive = FALSE ) ############################## # Posterior predictive check # ##############################  abc_posterior_predictive_check(   config = sim_config, # Simulation configuration   abc_result = abc_fit, # ABC fitting result   observed_df = observed_data,  # Observed dataset for comparison   n_conditions = 1, # Number of experimental conditions   n_trials_per_condition = 500, # Number of trials simulated per condition   rt_facet_x = c("choice"), # RT plot facet (x)   rt_facet_y = c(),   # RT plot facet (y)   accuracy_x = "rank_idx",  # Accuracy plot x-axis variable   accuracy_facet_x = c(), # Accuracy plot facet (x)   accuracy_facet_y = c()  # Accuracy plot facet (y) )

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: Beyond parameter estimation, eam package also supports model comparison within simulation-based inference framework. example, construct alternative model deliberately modifying prior specification relative starting point parameter Žü\rho, keeping components model unchanged. allows us isolate contribution parameter assumption overall model fit. Synthetic data generated alternative model, summary statistics pipeline applied ensure comparability across models. Model comparison conducted using abc_postpr() function, estimates posterior model probabilities based Approximate Bayesian Computation. Intuitively, procedure assesses model likely generated observed data comparing proximity simulated summary statistics model target summary statistics, given fixed tolerance level. resulting posterior probabilities indicate relative likelihood observed data generated candidate model. probabilities can summarized using Bayes factors, quantify strength evidence favor one model another. Conventionally, Bayes factors greater 3 smaller 1/3 interpreted providing substantial evidence one model relative competitor.
code: # Deliberately change the rho in the alternative model prior_formulas_alt <- list(   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(1, 5),   # Drift rate   V_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Relative starting point   rho_0 ~ 0.7 )  sim_config_alt <- sim_config sim_config_alt$prior_formulas <- prior_formulas_alt  # Run the simulation sim_output_alt <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config_alt )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics for the alternative model simulation_sumstat_alt <- map_by_condition(   sim_output_alt,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )  abc_input_alt <- build_abc_input(   simulation_output = sim_output_alt,   simulation_summary = simulation_sumstat_alt,   target_summary = target_sumstat,   param = c("A_0", "V_0", "ndt_0") )  # Run the model comparison postpr_result <- abc_postpr(   sumstats = list(     abc_input$sumstat,     abc_input_alt$sumstat   ),   target = abc_input$target,   tol = 0.05,   method = "rejection" )  summary(postpr_result) #> Call:  #> abc::postpr(target = target, index = index, sumstat = sumstat,  #>     tol = 0.05, method = "rejection") #> Data: #>  postpr.out$values (500 posterior samples) #> Models a priori: #>  model_1, model_2 #> Models a posteriori: #>  model_1, model_2 #>  #> Proportion of accepted simulations (rejection): #> model_1 model_2  #>   0.902   0.098  #>  #> Bayes factors: #>         model_1 model_2 #> model_1  1.0000  9.2041 #> model_2  0.1086  1.0000

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: construct input matrix ZZ observed data, containing trial-level choice reaction time (RT). dataset organized column corresponds one trial. trial, first row gives choice outcome, coded -1 1, second row gives corresponding response time (rt). can think column pair observations: choice RT trial. format directly matches ABI input structure, Z fixed-dimensional matrix trial-level data, rows representing features (, choice RT) columns representing individual trials. structure used simulated observed datasets. function build_abi_input() aligns simulated data parameters consistent format training.
code: ##################### # abc model prepare # ##################### library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  Z_observed <- observed_data %>%   mutate(trial = row_number()) %>%   select(trial, choice, rt) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(choice, rt),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) %>%   pivot_wider(     names_from = trial,     values_from = value   )  Z_observed <- as.matrix(Z_observed[, -1])  head(Z_observed[,1:5]) #>             1        2        3         4        5 #> [1,] 1.000000 1.000000 1.000000 1.0000000 1.000000 #> [2,] 1.223133 1.065525 1.062939 0.8827105 1.490901  # Align the simulated and observed summary statistics abi_input <- build_abi_input(   sim_output,   theta = c(     "A_0", "V_0", "ndt_0"   ),  # model parameters to be inferred   Z = c(     "choice",     "rt"   ),    # observed data features used for inference   train_ratio = 0.8,  # proportion of simulated data used for training and testing (80% for training and 20% for testing)   n_test = 100   # number of samples reserved for cross validation )

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: train neural estimator learn mapping observed data ZZ model parameters ╬Ė\theta. use point estimator, directly predicts parameter values input data. input dimension d = 2 corresponds two observed variables per trial (choice RT). DeepSet encoder maps set trials fixed-length representation, transformed parameter estimates. output dimension equals number parameters (p = 3), parameter predicted dedicated output unit using softplus activation enforce valid (positive) values. training, obtain point estimates parameters observed dataset.
code: ######################## # Parameter estimation # ########################  # Fit abi model point_estimator <- "   d = 2    # dimension of data features   w = 32   # number of neurons in each hidden layer    # Layer to ensure valid estimates   final_layer = Parallel(       vcat,       Dense(w, 1, softplus), #first model parameter       Dense(w, 1, softplus), #second model parameter       Dense(w, 1, softplus)  #third model parameter      )    psi = Chain(Dense(d, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu))   phi = Chain(Dense(w, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu), final_layer)   deepset = DeepSet(psi, phi)   estimator = PointEstimator(deepset) "  trained_point_estimator <- abi_train(   estimator = point_estimator,   abi_input = abi_input,   epochs = 200,   stopping_epochs = 20,   verbose = FALSE ) # Point estimation check point_est <- abi_estimate(   trained_estimator = trained_point_estimator,   Z = Z_observed )  point_est #>        estimate #> A_0   2.0368655 #> V_0   1.0363948 #> ndt_0 0.4494624

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: evaluate parameter recovery using simulated datasets. function abi_assess() performs validation procedure : sampling datasets known parameters re-estimating parameters using trained estimator comparing estimated vs true parameters Good recovery indicated high correlation estimated true values estimates concentrated around identity line assess model fit generating simulated data using estimated parameters comparing observed data. allows us evaluate whether model reproduces RT distributions choice proportions
code: # Parameter recovery check assessment <- abi_assess(   trained_estimator = trained_point_estimator,   estimator_name = "NBE" ) #>  Running NBE...  plot_cv_recovery(assessment) ############################## # Posterior predictive check # ##############################  abi_posterior_predictive_check(   config = sim_config,   trained_estimator = trained_point_estimator,   estimator_type = "point",   observed_df = observed_data,   Z = Z_observed,   rt_facet_x = c("choice"),   rt_facet_y = c(),   accuracy_x = "",   accuracy_facet_x = c(),   accuracy_facet_y = c() )

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
code: ##################### # abc model prepare # #####################  Z_observed <- observed_data %>%   mutate(trial = row_number()) %>%   select(trial, choice, rt) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(choice, rt),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) %>%   pivot_wider(     names_from = trial,     values_from = value   )  Z_observed <- as.matrix(Z_observed[, -1])  # Align the simulated and observed summary statistics abi_input <- build_abi_input(   sim_output,   theta = c(     "A_0", "V_0", "ndt_0"   ),   Z = c(     "choice",     "rt"   ),   train_ratio = 0.8,   n_test = 100 )

title: Getting Started: A Minimal Working Example
text: Instead predicting single point estimate, learn full posterior distribution. input dimension d = 2 corresponds two observed variables per trial (choice RT). DeepSet encoder maps set trials fixed-length representation. output dimension equals number parameters (p = 3). Instead producing point estimates, normalizing flow used generate samples posterior distribution parameters. training, draw samples posterior distribution.
code: ######################## # Parameter estimation # ########################  # Fit abi model posterior_estimator <- "   d = 2    # dimension of data features   w = 32   # number of neurons in each hidden layer    # Layer to ensure valid estimates   final_layer = Parallel(       vcat,       Dense(w, 1, softplus), #first model parameter       Dense(w, 1, softplus), #second model parameter       Dense(w, 1, softplus)  #third model parameter     )    psi = Chain(Dense(d, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu))   phi = Chain(Dense(w, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu), final_layer)   deepset = DeepSet(psi, phi)   w = 3 #number of model parameter   q = NormalisingFlow(w, w)   estimator = PosteriorEstimator(q, deepset) "  trained_posterior_estimator <- abi_train(   estimator = posterior_estimator,   abi_input = abi_input,   epochs = 200,   stopping_epochs = 20,   verbose = FALSE ) # Posterior sample estimation check posterior_samples <- abi_sample_posterior(   trained_estimator = trained_posterior_estimator,   Z = Z_observed,   N = 1000 )  # Summarise posterior parameters posterior_summary <- summarise_posterior_parameters(posterior_samples) print(posterior_summary) #>   dataset_id parameter      mean    median ci_lower_0.025 ci_upper_0.975 #> 1          1       A_0 2.0424385 2.0357143      1.7971618      2.3020147 #> 2          1       V_0 0.9913434 0.9835609      0.7950293      1.2071608 #> 3          1     ndt_0 0.4821002 0.4846332      0.3948782      0.5595917

title: Models in eam
text: diffusion decision model (DDM) double-boundary single-accumulator EAM Gaussian noise. moment--moment change accumulated evidence x(t)x(t) expressed : dx(t)=vŌŗģdt+ŽēŌŗģdWt,   dx(t) = v \cdot dt + \omega \cdot dW_t, dWtŌł╝N(0,dt),   dW_t \sim N(0,\, dt),  diffusion noise term dWtdW_t time step drawn Gaussian distribution mean 0 variance dtdt. diffusion scaling parameter Žē\omega typically fixed 1. starting point zz DDM often expressed proportion decision boundary aa: z=ŽüŌŗģ,ŽüŌłł[0,1],   z = \rho \cdot , \qquad \rho \[0,\, 1],  Žü\rho denotes relative bias toward upper boundary aa. larger Žü\rho places starting point closer upper boundary, making easier faster process reach , increasing probability correct response. free parameters model thus include drift rate vv, decision boundary aa, starting point zz (relative bias Žü\rho), non-decision time t0t_0. Beyond core parameters, DDM also incorporates three sources -trial variability: variability drift rate (svs_v), starting point (szs_z), non-decision time (st0s_{t0}). components optional, yet including enables model better capture empirical RT distributions, explain ŌĆ£fast errorsŌĆØ, separate within-trial noise -trial variability.
code: # Load necessary packages library(eam)  # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 1  prior_params <- tibble::tibble(   n_items = n_items )  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Drift rate   V_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   S_V ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   S_ndt ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Relative starting point   Z_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-0.5, 0.5),   S_Z ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.3) )  # Specify the between-trial components (optional) between_trial_formulas <- list(   # Between trial variability in drift rate   V_var ~ distributional::dist_normal(0, S_V),   # Between trial variability in non-decision time   ndt_var ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-S_ndt, S_ndt),   # Between trial variability in starting point   Z_var ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-S_Z, S_Z) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Relative starting point   Z ~ Z_0 + Z_var,   # Decision boundary   A_upper ~ A_0, # upper    A_lower ~ -A_0, # lower   # Drift rate   V ~ V_0 + V_var,   # Non-decision time   ndt ~ ndt_0 + ndt_var )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "ddm",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(choice == 1) / sum(!is.na(choice)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "choice"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

code: # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 2  prior_params <- tibble::tibble(   n_items = n_items )  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Drift rate   V_acc1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   V_acc2 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Leakage parameter   k ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.5),   # Inhibition strength   beta ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.5) )  # Specify the between-trial components between_trial_formulas <- list( )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A ~ A_0,   # Drift rate   V ~ c(V_acc1, V_acc2) )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = 1,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "lca",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config ) # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(item_idx == 1) / sum(!is.na(item_idx)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "item_idx"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

title: Models in eam
text: linear ballistic accumulator model single-boundary multiple-accumulator EAM non-decision noise. Without stochastic fluctuations moment moment, accumulated evidence evolves straight line slope equal drift rate viv_i, response RT determined first accumulator reach threshold: dx(t)=viŌŗģdt.     dx(t) = v_i \cdot dt.  account -trial variability observed response RTs, LBA introduces two additional random components. First, trial, accumulator begins random starting point ziz_i sampled uniform distribution U(0,Z)U(0, Z), capturing variability initial bias. Second, trial, drift rate accumulator drawn Gaussian distribution mean ViV_i variance Žä\tau, representing variability quality evidence: viŌł╝N(Vi,Žä).     v_i \sim N(V_i,\ \tau).  model identifiability, Žä\tau typically fixed constant, constraints imposed mean drift rates (e.g., V1+V2=1V_1 + V_2 = 1). free parameters model thus include mean drift rate accumulator ViV_i, decision boundary aa, upper bound starting point ZZ, non-decision time t0t_0. design gives LBA greater flexibility capturing error RT distributions allows straightforward extension tasks two options simply adding accumulators.
code: # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 2  prior_params <- tibble::tibble(   n_items = n_items )  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Drift rate   V_mu_acc1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   V_mu_acc2 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Upper limit of starting point   Z_upper ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.5) )  # Specify the between-trial components between_trial_formulas <- list(   V_acc1 ~ max(distributional::dist_normal(mean = V_mu_acc1, sd = 1), 1e-5),   V_acc2 ~ max(distributional::dist_normal(mean = V_mu_acc2, sd = 1), 1e-5),   Z_0   ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, Z_upper) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A ~ A_0,   # Drift rate   V ~ c(V_acc1, V_acc2),   # Starting point   Z ~ Z_0 )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     0 * rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = 1,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "lba",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(item_idx == 1) / sum(!is.na(item_idx)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "item_idx"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

title: Models in eam
text: racing diffusion model single-boundary, multiple-accumulator EAM Gaussian noise. moment--moment change accumulated evidence, x(t)x(t), follows stochastic updating rule DDM; key difference lies incorrect responses determined, namely allowing multiple accumulators ``raceŌĆÖŌĆÖ toward upper decision boundary, first cross dictating response RT. Due concerns parameter recovery, RDM avoids estimating -trial variability drift rates (svs_v) still allowing starting points (szs_z) vary across trials improve fit empirical RT distributions.

1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(item_idx == 1) / sum(!is.na(item_idx)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "item_idx"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

title: Models in eam
text: Levy flight model (LFM) extends traditional DDM different direction replacing Gaussian diffusion noise Levy ╬▒\alpha-stable distribution, allowing occasional large evidence jumps capture heavy-tailed RT distributions. double-boundary single-accumulator EAM Levy ╬▒\alpha-stable noise. moment--moment change accumulated evidence x(t)x(t) thus follows rules: dx(t)=vŌŗģdt+sŌŗģdL╬▒(t),   dx(t) = v \cdot dt + s \cdot dL_{\alpha}(t), dL╬▒(t)Ōł╝S╬▒(╬│,╬Ą,╬╝),   dL_{\alpha}(t) \sim S_{\alpha}(\gamma,\ \varepsilon,\ \mu),  S╬▒(Ōŗģ)S_{\alpha}(\cdot) denotes Levy ╬▒\alpha-stable distribution parameterized : ╬▒Ōłł(0,2]\alpha \(0,2]: stability index controlling tail heaviness ╬│Ōłł[ŌłÆ1,1]\gamma \[-1,1]: skewness parameter (╬│=0\gamma = 0 indicates symmetry) ╬Ą>0\varepsilon > 0: scale parameter ╬╝ŌłłŌäØ\mu \\mathbb{R}: location parameter LFM, stability parameter ╬▒\alpha free estimated data, whereas skewness (╬│\gamma), scale (╬Ą\varepsilon), location (╬╝\mu) fixed. Thus, free parameters LFM include drift rate single accumulator vv, decision boundary aa, starting point zz, non-decision time t0t_0, stability parameter ╬▒\alpha. LFM allows -trial variability drift rates (svs_v), starting points (szs_z), non-decision time (st0s_{t0}).
code: # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 1  prior_params <- tibble::tibble(   n_items = n_items )  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Drift rate   V_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   S_V ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   S_ndt ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Relative starting point   Z_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-0.5, 0.5),   S_Z ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.3),   # Stability parameter   alpha    ~ distributional::dist_uniform(1, 2) )  # Specify the between-trial components (optional) between_trial_formulas <- list(   # Between trial variability in drift rate   V_var ~ distributional::dist_normal(0, S_V),   # Between trial variability in non-decision time   ndt_var ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-S_ndt, S_ndt),   # Between trial variability in starting point   Z_var ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-S_Z, S_Z) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Relative starting point   Z ~ Z_0 + Z_var,   # Decision boundary   A_upper ~ A_0, # upper    A_lower ~ -A_0, # lower   # Drift rate   V ~ V_0 + V_var,   # Non-decision time   ndt ~ ndt_0 + ndt_var )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   alpha <- context$alpha   function(n, dt) sqrt(dt) * stabledist::rstable(n, alpha = alpha, beta = 0, gamma = 1 / sqrt(2), delta = 0) }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "lfm",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(choice == 1) / sum(!is.na(choice)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "choice"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

title: Models in eam
text: simulate multi-response EAM, introduced two additional components: position-dependent decision boundary function item-dependent drift rate function. First, decision boundary modeled linear increasing function output position oo: (o)=a0+a1Ōŗģo,   (o) = a_{0} + a_{1}\cdot o,  a0a_{0} represents decision boundary first-retrieved item a1>0a_{1} > 0 captures rate boundary increases across retrieval positions. recall episode progresses, criterion terminating evidence accumulation becomes stringent. increase may arise increased caution memory retrieval. Importantly, boundary linked output position rather item index, items lower drift rates may reach lower boundary chance. Second, drift rates specified linear decreasing function item index ii: v()=v0+v1Ōŗģ,   v() = v_{0} + v_{1}\cdot , v0v_{0} represents drift rate first item v1<0v_{1} < 0 reflects decline evidence strength across positions. intended capture reduction available memory cues retrieval strength later items, pattern commonly observed free recall tasks. following example use DDM template extend multi-response version:
code: # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 3  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   n_items ~ 3,   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   A_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Drift rate   V_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   V_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.3),   S_V ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   S_ndt ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Relative starting point   Z_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-0.5, 0.5),   S_Z ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 0.3) )  # Specify the between-trial components (optional) between_trial_formulas <- list(   # Between trial variability in drift rate   V_var ~ distributional::dist_normal(0, S_V),   # Between trial variability in non-decision time   ndt_var ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-S_ndt, S_ndt),   # Between trial variability in starting point   Z_var ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-S_Z, S_Z) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Relative starting point   Z ~ Z_0 + Z_var,   # Decision boundary   A_upper ~ (A_0 + seq(1, n_items) * A_1), # upper    A_lower ~ -(A_0 + seq(1, n_items) * A_1), # lower   # Drift rate   V ~ pmax(V_0 - seq(1, n_items) * V_1 + V_var, 1e-5),   # Non-decision time   ndt ~ ndt_0 + ndt_var )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "ddm",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx","rank_idx"),     acc = sum(choice == 1) / sum(!is.na(choice)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "rank_idx","choice"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

title: Models in eam
text: two general strategies incorporating covariates models. first approach, model parameters allowed vary systematically function group trial-level covariates. Specifically, parameters generated according ╬Ėi=╬Ė0+╬ĖcovXi,   \theta_i = \theta_0 + \theta_{\mathrm{cov}}\, X_i,  ╬Ė\theta denotes model parameters (e.g., drift rate, decision boundary, non-decision time), XiX_i covariate value trial ii, ╬Ė0\theta_0 baseline (intercept) parameter, ╬Ėcov\theta_{\mathrm{cov}} quantifies effect covariate. formulation flexible accommodates continuous discrete covariates. using simulation-based inference, choice summary statistics must reflect structure covariate. continuous covariates, summary statistics include estimated regression intercept slope capturing relationship response times (RTs) covariate. discrete covariates, summary statistics typically consist RT quantiles computed separately covariate level, together accuracy measures double-boundary EAMs. second approach, continuous covariates trial-level neural signals injected directly accumulation process modulating model parameters. Specifically, package allows users link trial-level neural measurements EAM parameters latent-variable formulation. scaling parameter ss introduced map neural signal onto appropriate parameter scale, variance term Žä2\tau^{2} captures discrepancy observed neural signal latent parameter value. formulation enables joint modeling neural behavioral data, allows formal tests whether incorporating neural information leads improved model performance. Formally, trial-level neural signal PiP_i (e.g., EEG fMRI measurements) linked corresponding parameter ╬Ėi\theta_i via ╬ĖiŌł╝­ØÆ®(sŌŗģPi,Žä2).   \theta_i \sim \mathcal{N}\bigl(s \cdot P_i,\, \tau^{2}\bigr). Reference: Ratcliff, R. (1978). theory memory retrieval. Psychological Review, 85 (2), 59-108. https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-295X.85.2.59 Usher, M., & McClelland, J. L. (2001). time course perceptual choice: leaky, competing accumulator model. Psychological Review, 108 (3), 550-592. https://doi.org/10.1037/0033-295X.108.3.550 Brown, S. D., & Heathcote, . (2008). simplest complete model choice response time: Linear ballistic accumulation. Cognitive Psychology, 57 (3), 153-178. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cogpsych.2007.12.002 Tillman, G., Van Zandt, T., & Logan, G. D. (2020). Sequential sampling models without random -trial variability: racing diffusion model speeded decision making. Psychonomic Bulletin & Review, 27 (5), 911-936. https://doi.org/10.3758/s13423-020-01719-6 Wieschen, E. M., Voss, ., & Radev, S. T. (2020). Jumping conclusion? Levy flight model decision making. Quantitative Methods Psychology, 16 (2), 120-132. https://doi.org/10.20982/tqmp.16.2.p120
code: # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 1  # Set the covariate cov1 <- rbinom(100, 1, 0.5)  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   n_items ~ 1,   # Decision boundary   A_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   A_beta_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-1, 1),   # Drift rate   V_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   V_beta_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-1, 1),   # Non-decision time   ndt_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   ndt_beta_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-1, 1),   # Relative starting point   Z_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-0.5, 0.5),   Z_beta_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-0.5, 0.5) )  # Specify the between-trial components (optional) between_trial_formulas <- list(   # Covariate   rlang::expr(cov1 ~ !!cov1) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Relative starting point   Z ~ Z_beta_0 + cov1 * Z_beta_1,   # Decision boundary   A_upper ~ (A_beta_0 + cov1 * A_beta_1), # upper    A_lower ~ -(A_beta_0  + cov1 * A_beta_1), # lower   # Drift rate   V ~ pmax(V_beta_0 + cov1 * V_beta_1, 1e-5),   # Non-decision time   ndt ~ pmax(ndt_beta_0 + cov1 * ndt_beta_1, 1e-5) )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "ddm",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx","rank_idx","cov1"),     acc = sum(choice == 1) / sum(!is.na(choice)),   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "rank_idx","choice","cov1"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.005, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7 ,0.9, 0.995))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) ) # Set the number of accumulators in the model n_items <- 1  # Set the covariate cov1 <- rnorm(100, 1, 0.5)  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   n_items ~ 1,   # Decision boundary   A_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   # Drift rate   V_coef ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3),   V_sigma ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Non-decision time   ndt_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0, 1),   # Relative starting point   Z_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-0.5, 0.5) )  # Specify the between-trial components (optional) between_trial_formulas <- list(   # Covariate   rlang::expr(cov1 ~ !!cov1),   V_latent ~ V_coef * stats::rnorm(1, mean = cov1, sd = V_sigma) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   # Relative starting point   Z ~ Z_0,   # Decision boundary   A_upper ~ (A_0), # upper    A_lower ~ -(A_0), # lower   # Drift rate   V ~ pmax(V_latent, 1e-5),   # Non-decision time   ndt ~ ndt_0 )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }  sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   # Pass the model information   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   # Specify the simulation conditions and number of trials   n_conditions = 1000,   n_trials_per_condition = 100,   # Specify the number of accumulators and the number of recorded accumulators   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   # Specify the total elapsed time and time step   max_t = 10,   dt = 0.001,   # Specify the noise structure   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   # Specify the model type   model = "ddm",   # Specify the parallel computing settings   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )  # Define the summary procedure summary_pipe <-   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx"),     acc = sum(choice == 1) / sum(!is.na(choice)),     lm_coef = lm(rt ~ cov1)$coefficients   ) +   summarise_by(     .by = c("condition_idx", "choice"),     rt_quantiles = quantile(rt, probs = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9))   )  # Calculate simulated summary statistics simulation_sumstat <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = TRUE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) summary_pipe(cond_df) )

title: An Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference for Free Recall
text: details model listed : Priors global parameters specify weakly informative priors global parameters governing decision boundary, drift rate, non-decision time, -trial variability: A╬▓0Ōł╝Uniform(0.5,5.0),A╬▓1Ōł╝Uniform(0.01,1.0),V╬▓0Ōł╝Uniform(0.5,3.0),V╬▓1Ōł╝Uniform(0.01,0.3),VŽāŌł╝Uniform(0.01,1.0),ndt╬▓0Ōł╝Uniform(ŌłÆ1,1).   \begin{aligned}   A_{\beta_0} &\sim \text{Uniform}(0.5, 5.0), \\   A_{\beta_1} &\sim \text{Uniform}(0.01, 1.0), \\   V_{\beta_0} &\sim \text{Uniform}(0.5, 3.0), \\   V_{\beta_1} &\sim \text{Uniform}(0.01, 0.3), \\   V_{\sigma} &\sim \text{Uniform}(0.01, 1.0), \\   ndt_{\beta_0} &\sim \text{Uniform}(-1, 1).   \end{aligned} -trial variability capture variabilities recall trajectories recall strategies across trials , introduce -trial variability drift rate: VvarŌł╝­ØÆ®(0,VŽā).   V_{\text{var}} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, V_{\sigma}). Item-level parameterization item-level, different model parameters linked different structural aspects task. Specifically, decision boundary assumed vary , whereas drift rate varies . Formally, define =A╬▓0+output positionŌŗģA╬▓1,V=maxŌüĪ(V╬▓0ŌłÆitem indexŌŗģV╬▓1+Vvar,╬Ė)   \begin{aligned}   &= A_{\beta_0} + \text{output position} \cdot A_{\beta_1}, \\   V &= \max\!\left( V_{\beta_0} - \text{item index} \cdot V_{\beta_1} + V_{\text{var}}, \;                     \theta \right)   \end{aligned}  ╬Ė>0\theta > 0 small constant ensuring positivity drift rate. Evidence accumulation dynamics Within trial, evidence evolves according Wiener diffusion process: dX(t)=Vidt+ŽādW(t),dW(t)Ōł╝­ØÆ®(0,dt),   dX(t) = V_i \, dt + \sigma \, dW(t),   \qquad   dW(t) \sim \mathcal{N}(0, dt), response generated one accumulator first reaches decision boundary AiA_i. observed response time item ii given RTi=Tdecision,+ndti.   RT_i = T_{\text{decision},} + ndt_i .

title: An Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference for Free Recall
text: First, load required packages. , specify model configuration according setup described .
code: # Load necessary packages library(eam) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  # Set a random seed for reproducibility set.seed(1) ####################### # Model specification # ####################### # Define the number of accumulators n_items <- 7  # Specify the prior distributions for free parameters prior_formulas <- list(   n_items ~ 7,   # parameters with distributions   A_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.50, 5.00),   A_beta_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.01, 1.00),   # V   V_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.5, 3.00),   V_beta_1 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.01, 0.3),   V_sigma ~ distributional::dist_uniform(0.01, 1.0),   # ndt   ndt_beta_0 ~ distributional::dist_uniform(-1, 1),   # noise param   noise_coef ~ 1 )  # Specify the between-trial components  between_trial_formulas <- list(   # random group binomial   V_var ~ distributional::dist_normal(0, V_sigma) )  # Specify the item-level parameters item_formulas <- list(   A ~ A_beta_0 + seq(1, n_items) * A_beta_1,   V ~ pmax(V_beta_0 - seq(1, n_items) * V_beta_1 + V_var, 1e-5),   ndt ~ ndt_beta_0 )  # Specify the diffusion noise noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) {     rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = sqrt(dt))   } }

title: An Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference for Free Recall
text: next generate simulated datasets specified model. Depending computational resources, step may take approximately 30 minutes 1 hour parallelization enabled.
code: #################### # Simulation setup # #################### sim_config <- new_simulation_config(   prior_formulas = prior_formulas,   between_trial_formulas = between_trial_formulas,   item_formulas = item_formulas,   n_conditions_per_chunk = NULL, # automatic chunking   n_conditions = 5000,   n_trials_per_condition = 1000,   n_items = n_items,   max_reached = n_items,   max_t = 60,   dt = 0.001,   noise_mechanism = "add",   noise_factory = noise_factory,   model = "ddm",   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL, # Will use default: detectCores() - 1   rand_seed = NULL # Will use default random seed )  ################## # Run simulation # ################## sim_output <- run_simulation(   config = sim_config )

title: An Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference for Free Recall
text: illustration purposes, use data one representative participant PEERS dataset observed data example.
code: ####################### # Load Observed data  # #######################  observed_data <- read.csv("./30-empirical-example/example_data.csv") observed_data$condition_idx <- 1

what: Step four: Prepare inputs for inference
title: An Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference for Free Recall
text: construct input amortized Bayesian inference specifying model parameters estimated (╬Ė\theta) observed variables (ZZ). model trained simulated datasets learns mapping data parameters. , use reaction times (rt) input representation split simulated data training test sets.
code: ##################### # abi model prepare # #####################  Z_observed <- observed_data %>%   select(session_list, rank_idx, rt) %>%   pivot_wider(     names_from = session_list,     values_from = rt   ) %>%   arrange(rank_idx)  Z_observed <- as.matrix(Z_observed[, -1]) rownames(Z_observed) <- paste0("rank_", observed_data$rank_idx %>% unique(), "_item_idx")  abi_input <- build_abi_input(   sim_output,   theta = c(     "A_beta_0", "A_beta_1", "V_beta_0", "V_beta_1", "V_sigma", "ndt_beta_0"   ),   Z = c(     "rt"   ),   train_ratio = 0.8,   n_test = 100 )

title: An Empirical Example: Simulation-Based Inference for Free Recall
text: perform amortized Bayesian inference estimate posterior distributions model parameters.
code: ############################################# # Model validation and parameter estimation # #############################################  posterior_estimator <- "   d = 7    # dimension of each replicate   w = 32   # number of neurons in each hidden layer    # Layer to ensure valid estimates   final_layer = Parallel(       vcat,       Dense(w, 1, softplus),       Dense(w, 1, softplus),       Dense(w, 1, softplus),       Dense(w, 1, softplus),       Dense(w, 1, softplus),       Dense(w, 1, softplus)     )    psi = Chain(Dense(d, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu))   phi = Chain(Dense(w, w, relu), Dense(w, w, relu), final_layer)   deepset = DeepSet(psi, phi)   w = 6   q = NormalisingFlow(w, w)   estimator = PosteriorEstimator(q, deepset) "  trained_posterior_estimator <- abi_train(   estimator = posterior_estimator,   abi_input = abi_input,   epochs = 200,   stopping_epochs = 50,   verbose = FALSE )  # Sample from posterior distribution posterior_samples <- abi_sample_posterior(   trained_estimator = trained_posterior_estimator,   Z = Z_observed,   N = 1000 )  # Summarise posterior parameters for each dataset posterior_summary <- summarise_posterior_parameters(posterior_samples) print(posterior_summary)  # Cross-validation posterior_samples <- abi_sample_posterior(   trained_estimator = trained_posterior_estimator,   N = 1000 )  plot_cv_recovery(   posterior_samples,   trained_estimator = trained_posterior_estimator )

Reference: Kahana, M. J., Lohnas, L. J., Healey, M. K., Aka, ., Broitman, . W., Crutchley, P., Crutchley, E., Alm, K. H., Katerman, B. S., Miller, N. E., Kuhn, J. R., Li, Y., Long, N. M., Miller, J., Paron, M. D., Pazdera, J. K., Pedisich, ., Rudoler, J. H., & Weidemann, C. T. (2024). Penn Electrophysiology Encoding Retrieval Study. Journal Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, Cognition, 50(9), 1421-1443. https://doi.org/10.1037/xlm0001319
code: ############################## # Posterior predictive check # ##############################  abi_posterior_predictive_check(   config = sim_config,   trained_estimator = trained_posterior_estimator,   estimator_type = "posterior",   observed_df = observed_data,   Z = Z_observed,   posterior_dataset_id = 1,   posterior_n_samples = 1000,   rt_facet_x = c("rank_idx"),   rt_facet_y = c(),   accuracy_x = "rank_idx",   accuracy_facet_x = c("ndt_beta_0"),   accuracy_facet_y = c() )

code: evidence <- 0 dt <- 0.1 A <- 0 noise_fun <- function() 1 trace <- data.frame(step_idx = 0, evidence = evidence)  for (step_idx in 1:2) {     direction <- if (step_idx %% 2 == 1) -1 else 1     noise <- noise_fun()     evidence <- evidence + direction * abs(noise)     trace <- rbind(trace, data.frame(step_idx = step_idx, evidence = evidence))      if (evidence >= A) {       rt <- step_idx * dt       break     } }  list(rt = rt, evidence = evidence) #> $rt #> [1] 0.2 #>  #> $evidence #> [1] 0  plot(   trace$step_idx,   trace$evidence,   type = "b",   xlab = "Step",   ylab = "Evidence",   main = "Alternating evidence path" ) abline(h = A, lty = 2)

text: ķéŻõ╣łńö©µłĘÕ”éõĮĢÕłżµ¢Ł AI ń┐╗Ķ»æńÜä cpp Õ«×ńÄ░µś»ÕÉ”Õ┐ĀÕ«×õ║ÄĶć¬ÕĘ▒ńÜäµäÅÕøŠÕæó’╝¤µłæõ╗¼ÕÅ»õ╗źķĆÜĶ┐ćµĄŗĶ»ĢµØźĶ¦ŻÕå│ŃĆé ķĆÜÕĖĖ’╝īµłæõ╗¼ķØ×ÕĖĖńå¤µéēõĖĆõĖ¬µ©ĪÕ×ŗÕ£©õĖĆõ║øĶāĮµēŗń«ŚńÜäµØĪõ╗ČõĖŗńÜäńē╣Õ«ÜĶĪīõĖ║ŃĆéõŠŗÕ”é’╝ī Õ”éµ×£ÕÅ¬µ£ēõĖĆµŁź’╝īń╗ōµ×£µś»õ╗Ćõ╣ł’╝¤ Õ”éµ×£ÕÖ¬ķ¤│õĖ║ 0’╝īĶĆīÕø║Õ«ÜÕüÅń¦╗õĖĆÕ«Ü’╝īķéŻõ╣łÕÅŹÕ║öµŚČõ╝Üµś»õ╗Ćõ╣ł’╝¤ ķĆÜÕĖĖ’╝īµé©ÕÅ¬ķ£ĆĶ”üÕÉæ AI µÅÉõŠøÕćĀõĖ¬Ķ┐ÖµĀĘńÜäĶŠ╣ńĢīµØĪõ╗Č’╝īÕ«āÕ░▒ÕÅ»õ╗źÕĖ«µé©µŖŖÕ«āõ╗¼ÕåÖµłÉ r Ķ»ŁĶ©ĆńÜäµĄŗĶ»Ģńö©õŠŗŃĆéµé©ÕÅ¬ķ£ĆĶ”üµĄÅĶ¦łĶ┐Öõ║øµĄŗĶ»Ģ’╝īńĪ«Ķ«żÕ«āõ╗¼Ķ”åńø¢õ║åµé©µ£ĆÕģ│Õ┐āńÜäĶŠ╣ńĢīµØĪõ╗Č’╝īÕ╣ČÕÅŹµśĀõ║åµé©ńÜäńÉåĶ«║ńø┤Ķ¦ēŃĆéķĆÜĶ┐ćµĄŗĶ»Ģ’╝īµé©õĖŹķ£ĆĶ”üķĆÉĶĪīÕ«Īķśģ C++ õ╗ŻńĀü’╝īÕ░▒ĶāĮÕ»╣Õ«āńÜäĶĪīõĖ║µ£ēõ┐ĪÕ┐āŃĆé Õ»╣Ķ┐ÖõĖ¬ŌĆ£õĖĆõĖŖõĖĆõĖŗŌĆØÕŹĢńĢīµ©ĪÕ×ŗ’╝īõĖżõĖ¬µ£ĆÕ░Å case ÕĘ▓ń╗ÅĶČ│Õż¤Ķ»┤µśÄµĀĖÕ┐āĶ¦äÕłÖŃĆéĶ┐ÖķćīńÜäµĄŗĶ»ĢÕł╗µäÅ monkey patch õ║å noise_fun’╝īĶ«®Õ«āµĆ╗µś»Ķ┐öÕø× 1ŃĆéÕøĀµŁżõĖżµŁźÕø×Õł░ 0 µś»ńĪ«Õ«ÜµĆ¦µĄŗĶ»Ģń╗ōĶ«║’╝øÕ”éµ×£µé©õĮ┐ńö©ķÜÅµ£║ noise’╝īÕ░▒õĖŹÕ║öĶ»źµĄŗĶ»ĢÕŹĢµ¼ĪĶĘ»ÕŠäÕø×Õł░ 0’╝īĶĆīÕ║öĶ»źµĄŗĶ»ĢÕżÜµ¼ĪķćŹÕżŹÕÉÄńÜäÕØćÕĆ╝µś»ÕÉ”µÄźĶ┐æ 0ŃĆé µé©ÕÅ¬ķ£ĆĶ”üÕÉæ AI µÅÅĶ┐░Ķ┐Öõ║øµĄŗĶ»ĢµĪłõŠŗ’╝īÕ«āÕ░▒Õ║öĶ»źĶāĮĶć¬ÕŖ©ńö¤µłÉõ╗źõĖŗõ╗ŻńĀü’╝īÕ╣ČµŖŖÕ«āµöŠÕł░µŁŻńĪ«ńÜäµ¢ćõ╗ČÕż╣ķćīŃĆéõŠŗÕ”é’╝īÕ£©µłæõ╗¼ńÜäĶ┐ÖõĖ¬õŠŗÕŁÉõĖŁ’╝īAI ÕłøÕ╗║õ║åõĖĆõĖ¬Õģ©µ¢░ńÜäµĄŗĶ»Ģµ¢ćõ╗Č tests/testthat/test-custom-model.RŃĆéÕ╣ČµĘ╗ÕŖĀĶ┐ÉĶĪīõ║åõ╗źõĖŗµĄŗĶ»Ģõ╗ŻńĀü’╝Ü Ķ┐Öõ║øõ╗ŻńĀüµś»ķØ×ÕĖĖÕ«╣µśōĶ»╗µćéńÜä’╝īÕÅ¬Ķ”üĶ┐Öõ║øµĄŗĶ»ĢķĆÜĶ┐ćõ║å’╝īµłæõ╗¼Õ¤║µ£¼Õ░▒ĶāĮńøĖõ┐Īõ╗¢ńÜäń┐╗Ķ»æµ▓Īµ£ēÕć║ķöÖŃĆé Ķ┐Öµś»õĖĆõĖ¬µŁŻńĪ«ńÜäµ©ĪÕ×ŗŃĆé õĖĆõĖ¬ AI ķ£ĆĶ”üµ│©µäÅńÜäÕ░Åń╗åĶŖé’╝ÜĶ┐Öõ║øµĄŗĶ»ĢÕ║öÕĮōÕāÅÕģČõ╗¢ backend µĄŗĶ»ĢõĖĆµĀĘ’╝īńø┤µÄźµĄŗĶ»Ģ accumulate_evidence_*()’╝īĶĆīõĖŹµś»ÕģłµĄŗĶ»Ģ configŃĆürouter µł¢Õ«īµĢ┤ simulation pipeŃĆéĶ┐Öõ║øķā©Õłåńö▒ÕīģńÜäÕ╝ĆÕÅæĶĆģń╗┤µŖż’╝īńö©µłĘķĆÜÕĖĖõĖŹķ£ĆĶ”üõ┐«µö╣µł¢µĄŗĶ»ĢÕ«āõ╗¼ŃĆé
code: # ÕÅ¬µ£ēõĖĆµŁź’╝Üõ╗Ä 0 Õć║ÕÅæ’╝īń¼¼õĖĆµŁźÕÉæõĖŗÕł░ -1’╝īõĖŹĶāĮĶŠŠÕł░ A = 0ŃĆé one_step <- accumulate_evidence_custom_model(   A = c(0),   Z = c(0),   ndt = c(0),   max_t = 0.1,   dt = 0.1,   max_reached = 1,   noise_func = function(n, dt) rep(1, n) ) stopifnot(length(one_step$item_idx) == 0)  # õĖżµŁź’╝Üń¼¼õĖĆµŁź -1’╝īń¼¼õ║īµŁźÕø×Õł░ 0’╝īÕ╣ČĶŠŠÕł░ A = 0ŃĆé two_steps <- accumulate_evidence_custom_model(   A = c(0),   Z = c(0),   ndt = c(0),   max_t = 0.2,   dt = 0.1,   max_reached = 1,   noise_func = function(n, dt) rep(1, n) ) stopifnot(two_steps$item_idx == 1) stopifnot(two_steps$evidence == 0)

text: Õł░ńø«ÕēŹõĖ║µŁó’╝īµé©ÕĘ▓ń╗ÅÕ«īµłÉõ║åµ£ĆÕŹ▒ķÖ®ńÜäķā©ÕłåŃĆéÕĮōõĮĀńÜäµĄŗĶ»ĢķĆÜĶ┐ćµŚČ’╝īµé©Õ░▒ÕÅ»õ╗źĶć¬õ┐ĪÕ£░Ķ»┤’╝īµłæÕĘ▓ń╗Åµŗźµ£ēõ║åõĖĆõĖ¬Õ«īÕģ©ń¼”ÕÉłµłæµÅÅĶ┐░µ©ĪÕ×ŗńÜäÕÉÄń½»õ║åŃĆéõĖŗõĖĆµŁź’╝īÕÅ¬ķ£ĆĶ”üµŖŖĶ┐ÖõĖ¬µĀĖÕ┐āµ©ĪÕ×ŗõĖÄµłæõ╗¼ńÜäµĪåµ×ČĶ┐×µÄźĶĄĘµØź’╝īµé©Õ░▒ÕÅ»õ╗źÕ╝ĆÕ¦ŗõĮ┐ńö©Õ«āõ║åŃĆé õĖŗķØóµś»Ķ┐ÖõĖĆĶ┐ćń©ŗńÜäµ”éĶ┐░ŃĆé Õ«×ķÖģõĖŖ’╝īµé©õĖŹķ£ĆĶ”üńÉåĶ¦Żõ╗¢õ╗¼ŃĆéńø┤µÄźĶ«® AI µīēńģ¦µłæõ╗¼ńÜäÕ╗║Ķ««µÄźõĖŖµÄźÕÅŻÕŹ│ÕÅ»ŃĆé AI Õ£©õĖŖõĖŗµ¢ćµśÄńĪ«ńÜäµāģÕåĄõĖŗ’╝īķĆÜÕĖĖÕŹüÕłåµōģķĢ┐Ķ┐Öń▒╗µÄźń║┐ńÜäÕĘźõĮ£ŃĆéµöŠÕ┐āÕ£░õ║żń╗ÖÕ«āÕŹ│ÕÅ»ŃĆéõĖ║õ║åĶ«®µé©Õ«ēÕ┐ā’╝īµłæõ╗¼õ╣¤Õ£©µłæõ╗¼ńÜäµĪåµ×Čķćīµ£ēķØ×ÕĖĖõĖźµĀ╝ńÜäµĢ░µŹ«ń║”Õ«Ü’╝īõ╗╗õĮĢ AI õ║¦ńö¤ńÜäķØ×ÕÉłńÉåµĢ░µŹ«ķāĮÕ░åĶó½µŗÆń╗ØŃĆé Õ”éµ×£µé©ńø┤µÄźÕ¤║õ║ÄÕĮōÕēŹ placeholder õ┐«µö╣’╝īķĆÜÕĖĖÕÅ¬ķ£ĆĶ”üÕģ│µ│©õĖēõĖ¬Õ£░µ¢╣ŃĆé ń¼¼õĖĆ’╝īõ┐«µö╣ src/custom-model.cpp õĖŁńÜä accumulate_evidence_custom_model()ŃĆéĶ┐Öķćīµś»ń£¤µŁŻńÜäÕŹĢ trial ń¦»ń┤»Ķ┐ćń©ŗŃĆéµé©ÕÅ»õ╗źµø┐µŹóÕŠ¬ńÄ»õĖŁńÜäĶ»üµŹ«µø┤µ¢░Ķ¦äÕłÖŃĆüĶŠ╣ńĢīÕłżµ¢ŁŃĆüķóØÕż¢ĶŠōÕć║ÕłŚŃĆéÕ░ĮķćÅõ┐ØńĢÖµ£ĆÕ░Åµā»õŠŗÕłŚ’╝Ü item_idx rt ń¼¼õ║ī’╝īõ┐«µö╣ R/simulation_models.R õĖŁńÜä run_trial_custom_model()ŃĆéĶ┐ÖõĖĆÕ▒éĶ┤¤Ķ┤ŻµŖŖńö©µłĘÕ£© item_formulas õĖŁÕåÖńÜäÕÅéµĢ░ÕÉŹ’╝īµśÄńĪ«µśĀÕ░äÕł░ C++ ÕćĮµĢ░ÕÅéµĢ░ŃĆéõŠŗÕ”éÕĮōÕēŹńż║õŠŗĶ”üµ▒é’╝Ü ndt ÕÅ»ķĆē Z Õ”éµ×£µé©ńÜäµ©ĪÕ×ŗķ£ĆĶ”üµ¢░ÕÅéµĢ░’╝īõŠŗÕ”é leakŃĆüurgencyŃĆübias’╝īÕ░▒Õ£©Ķ┐ÖķćīµśŠÕ╝ÅĶ»╗ÕÅ¢ item_params$leakŃĆüitem_params$urgencyŃĆüitem_params$bias’╝īńäČÕÉÄõ╝Āń╗Ö C++ ÕćĮµĢ░ŃĆé ń¼¼õĖē’╝īõ┐«µö╣ tests/testthat/test-custom-model.RŃĆéµĄŗĶ»ĢÕ║öĶ»źńø┤µÄźĶ░āńö© accumulate_evidence_custom_model()’╝īÕģłĶ”åńø¢µ£ĆÕ░ÅŃĆüńĪ«Õ«ÜŃĆüĶāĮµēŗń«ŚńÜäĶĪīõĖ║ŃĆéconfigŃĆürouterŃĆüÕż¦Ķ¦äµ©Īµ©Īµŗ¤ÕÆīµŗ¤ÕÉłÕÅ»õ╗źµÖÜõĖĆńé╣ÕåŹń£ŗŃĆé ķĆÜÕĖĖõĖŹķ£ĆĶ”üõ┐«µö╣’╝Ü flatten_simulation_results() run_simulation() ńÜäµĢ░µŹ«ÕåÖÕć║ķĆ╗ĶŠæ build_abc_input() build_abi_input() plot_rt()

text: Õł░Ķ┐Öķćī’╝īµēĆµ£ēńÜäÕĘźõĮ£Õ░▒Õ«īµłÉõ║å’╝īõĖŗõĖĆµŁźÕ░▒µś»õĮ┐ńö©Õ«āõ║å’╝ü ÕĮōÕēŹ placeholder ÕĘ▓ń╗Åµ│©ÕåīõĖ║ "custom-model"’╝īÕÅ»õ╗źńø┤µÄźÕ£© config õĖŁõĮ┐ńö©ŃĆéÕøĀõĖ║Õ«āµś»ÕŹĢńĢīµ©ĪÕ×ŗ’╝īÕÉÄń╗Ł simulationŃĆüsummaryŃĆüABC/ABI ÕÆī posterior predictive ńÜäń«Īń║┐ÕÅ»õ╗źÕÅéńģ¦ńÄ░µ£ē DDM-1B µł¢ÕģČõ╗¢ tutorial’╝ÜµŖŖ model µŹóµłÉ "custom-model"’╝īµŖŖ item_formulas õĖŁńÜäÕÅéµĢ░µŹóµłÉ run_trial_custom_model() ķ£ĆĶ”üńÜäÕÅéµĢ░ÕŹ│ÕÅ»ŃĆé Ķ┐Öķćīń╗ÖõĖĆõĖ¬µ£ĆÕ░ÅµŻĆµ¤ź’╝īÕÅ¬ķ¬īĶ»ü custom backend ÕĘ▓ń╗ÅĶāĮĶ┐øÕģź run_simulation()’╝Ü Ķ┐ÖõĖ¬µ£ĆÕ░Å pipe ÕÅ¬ĶĘæõĖĆõĖ¬ conditionŃĆüÕŹüõĖ¬ trialŃĆüõĖĆõĖ¬ itemŃĆéÕ«āõĖŹµś»Õ«īµĢ┤ńÜä demo_abc.R’╝īõ╣¤õĖŹµČēÕÅŖµŗ¤ÕÉłŃĆéńĪ«Ķ«żÕ«āĶāĮĶĘæķĆÜõ╗źÕÉÄ’╝īÕÉÄń╗ŁÕ░▒ÕÅ»õ╗źÕø×Õł░ÕģČõ╗¢µĢÖń©ŗķćīńÜäµĀćÕćå pipe’╝ÜÕ«Üõ╣ē summary statistics’╝īµ×äķĆĀ build_abc_input() µł¢ build_abi_input()’╝īÕåŹń╗¦ń╗Łµŗ¤ÕÉłÕÆī posterior predictive checkŃĆé Ķ┐ÖõĖ¬õŠŗÕŁÉõĖŹµś»õĖ║õ║åµłÉõĖ║õĖĆõĖ¬ń¦æÕŁ”õĖŖµ£ēńö©ńÜäµ©ĪÕ×ŗ’╝īĶĆīµś»õĖ║õ║åµÅÉõŠøõĖĆõĖ¬Õ«īµĢ┤ŃĆüÕÅ»µĄŗĶ»ĢŃĆüÕÅ»µø┐µŹóńÜäķ¬©µ×ČŃĆéń£¤µŁŻõĮ┐ńö©µŚČ’╝īµé©Õ║öĶ»źµŖŖŌĆ£õĖĆõĖŖõĖĆõĖŗŌĆØńÜäĶ¦äÕłÖµø┐µŹóµłÉĶć¬ÕĘ▒ńÜäĶ»üµŹ«ń¦»ń┤»Ķ┐ćń©ŗŃĆé
code: noise_factory <- function(context) {   function(n, dt) rep(1, n) }  config <- new_simulation_config(   prior_formulas = list(),   between_trial_formulas = list(),   item_formulas = list(     A ~ 0,     ndt ~ 0   ),   n_conditions = 1,   n_trials_per_condition = 10,   n_items = 1,   max_reached = 1,   max_t = 0.2,   dt = 0.1,   noise_factory = noise_factory,   model = "custom-model" )  sim_output <- run_simulation(config) sim_output$open_dataset()

title: Developer Guide
text: project currently active development, contributions community welcome. maintain inclusive environment contributors around world, ensure discussions understandable maintainable many developers possible, please prefer using English opening issues, submitting pull requests, participating development discussions.

title: Developer Guide
text: vignettes involving actual computation execution, simulation tutorials, please adopt local pre-compilation approach accelerate GitHub Pages build process. corresponding file structure document follows. example, document named tutorial-:
code: . Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ vignettes Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ tutorial-a.Rmd.orig      # Pre-compilation source file (can be omitted for documents without computation) Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ tutorial-a.Rmd           # Final file generated from source Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ tutorial-a/              # Subdirectory for storing resources and pre-compiled figures

title: Developer Guide
text: First, vignettes/ directory, rename Rmd file requires pre-compilation *.Rmd.orig, example tutorial-.Rmd.orig. Ensure Rmd file, configure figure path subdirectory vignettes/ name, prefer using vector graphics formats (SVG) better scaling. example configuration: , local R environment, run appropriate line code generate final Rmd file: Alternatively, can manually modify run following code snippet compile specific Rmd file:
code: knitr::opts_chunk$set(   collapse = TRUE,   comment = "#>",   fig.path = "./tutorial-a/",   dev = "svg",   fig.ext = "svg" ) # In R environment source("scripts/knit_vignettes.R")  # Or in terminal Rscript scripts/knit_vignettes.R  # If you need to force recompile all scripts (not recommended) Rscript scripts/knit_vignettes.R false old <- getwd() setwd("vignettes")  knitr::knit(   "tutorial-a.Rmd.orig",   output = "tutorial-a.Rmd" )  setwd(old)

title: Developer Guide

text: Ķ┐Öõ╗Įµ¢ćµĪŻĶ»┤µśÄÕ£© eam õĖŁÕŖĀÕģźõĖĆõĖ¬µ¢░ńÜä evidence accumulation process µŚČ’╝īķ£ĆĶ”üń╗ÅĶ┐ćÕō¬õ║øµÄźÕÅŻÕ▒éŃĆéĶ┐ÖķćīńÜäŌĆ£µ¢░µ©ĪÕ×ŗŌĆØńē╣µīćń¦»ń┤»Ķ┐ćń©ŗµ£¼Ķ║½õĖŹÕÉī’╝ÜńŖČµĆüÕ”éõĮĢµø┤µ¢░ŃĆüĶŠ╣ńĢīÕ”éõĮĢÕłżµ¢ŁŃĆüń½×õ║ēµł¢µ│äµ╝ÅÕ”éõĮĢÕÅæńö¤ŃĆüµś»ÕÉ”ķ£ĆĶ”üķóØÕż¢ÕÅéµĢ░ńŁēŃĆéÕ«āõĖŹÕīģµŗ¼µ¢░ńÜäµĢ░µŹ«ń«Īń║┐ŃĆüµ¢░ńÜäĶŠōÕć║µĀ╝Õ╝Å’╝īµł¢µ¢░ńÜäÕÉÄÕżäńÉåÕŹÅĶ««ŃĆé ÕøĀµŁż’╝īõĖĆõĖ¬µ¢░ńÜä backend ÕÅ»õ╗źķ£ĆĶ”üµ¢░ńÜäĶŠōÕģźÕÅéµĢ░’╝īõ╣¤ÕÅ»õ╗źÕ£©ń╗ōµ×£õĖŁĶĪźÕģģĶć¬ÕĘ▒µ£ēńö©ńÜäķóØÕż¢ÕłŚ’╝øõĮåÕ«āÕ║öĶ»źķüĄÕ«łµ£ĆÕ░ÅĶŠōÕć║µā»õŠŗŃĆéÕÅ¬Ķ”üĶ┐Öõ║øµā»õŠŗÕłŚÕŁśÕ£©’╝īńÄ░µ£ē plotŃĆüsummaryŃĆüABC/ABI ńŁēµĢ░µŹ«ń«Īń║┐Õ░▒Õ║öÕĮōń╗¦ń╗ŁÕĘźõĮ£ŃĆéĶ┤Īńī«ĶĆģńÜäńø«µĀćµś»µŖŖµ¢░ńÜäń¦»ń┤»Ķ┐ćń©ŗµÄźÕģźńÄ░µ£ē simulation configŃĆüR wrapperŃĆüRcpp backend ÕÆīÕÉÄń╗ŁÕłåµ×Éń«Īń║┐’╝īĶĆīõĖŹµś»Ķ«®ńö©µłĘõĖ║õ║åÕ¤║µ£¼ÕĘźõĮ£µĄüÕŁ”õ╣ĀÕÅ”õĖĆÕźŚµĢ░µŹ«µĀ╝Õ╝ÅŃĆé Õ£©ńÄ░µ£ēÕĘźõĮ£µĄüķćī’╝īńö©µłĘķĆÜÕĖĖÕÅ¬ÕåÖõĖĆõ╗Į simulation config’╝Ü demo/demo_abc.R Õ▒Ģńż║õ║åĶ┐ÖõĖ¬Ķ░āńö©ķōŠÕ£©Õ«īµĢ┤ ABC ÕĘźõĮ£µĄüķćīńÜäõĮŹńĮ«’╝ÜÕģłķĆÜĶ┐ć new_simulation_config() Õ«Üõ╣ēµ©ĪÕ×ŗŃĆüÕÅéµĢ░Õģ¼Õ╝ÅŃĆüÕÖ¬ÕŻ░µ£║ÕłČÕÆīµ©Īµŗ¤Ķ¦äµ©Ī’╝øÕåŹńö© run_simulation() ńö¤µłÉµ©Īµŗ¤µĢ░µŹ«’╝øķÜÅÕÉÄµŖŖĶŠōÕć║µĢ┤ńÉåµłÉ summary statistics Õ╣Čõ║żń╗Ö ABC µł¢ posterior predictive checkŃĆéµē®Õ▒Ģµ¢░ń¦»ń┤»Ķ┐ćń©ŗµŚČ’╝īµ£ĆķćŹĶ”üńÜäÕÄ¤ÕłÖµś»’╝Üµ¢░ backend Õ║öĶ»źĶāĮĶó½Ķ┐ÖõĖ¬Ķ░āńö©ķōŠĶć¬ńäČõĮ┐ńö©’╝īĶĆīõĖŹµś»Ķ”üµ▒éńö©µłĘń╗ĢĶ┐ćńÄ░µ£ēķģŹńĮ«ń│╗ń╗¤ŃĆé

code: run_trial_my_model <- function(     trial_setting,     item_formulas,     n_items,     max_reached,     max_t,     dt,     noise_mechanism,     noise_factory,     trajectories = FALSE) {   item_params <- evaluate_with_dt(     item_formulas,     data = trial_setting,     n = n_items   )   noise_fun <- noise_factory(trial_setting)    Z <- if (is.null(item_params$Z)) rep(0, n_items) else item_params$Z    sim_result <- accumulate_evidence_my_model(     item_params$A,     item_params$V,     Z,     item_params$ndt,     max_t,     dt,     max_reached,     noise_fun   )    if (trajectories) {     sim_result$.item_params <- item_params   }    sim_result }

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Zhu, G., & Yang, G. (2026). Simulation-Based Evidence Accumulation Modeling Single- Multi-Response Tasks: eam Package. PsyArXiv. https://doi.org/10.31234/osf.io/dg7c2_v1
code: @Article{,   title = {Simulation-Based Evidence Accumulation Modeling for Single- and Multi-Response Tasks: The eam Package},   author = {Guangyu Zhu and Guang Yang},   year = {2026},   doi = {10.31234/osf.io/dg7c2_v1},   url = {https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/dg7c2_v1},   note = {PsyArXiv preprint},   journal = {PsyArXiv}, }

title: Evidence Accumulation Models
text: eam simulation-based evidence accumulation models analyzing responses reaction times single- multi-response tasks. package includes simulation engines five representative models: Diffusion Decision Model (DDM), Leaky Competing Accumulator (LCA), Linear Ballistic Accumulator (LBA), Racing Diffusion Model (RDM), L├®vy Flight Model (LFM), extends frameworks multi-response settings. package supports user-defined functions item-level parameterization incorporation covariates, enabling flexible customization development new model variants based existing architectures. Inference performed using simulation-based methods, including Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC) Amortized Bayesian Inference (ABI), allow parameter estimation without requiring tractable likelihood functions. addition core inference tools, package provides modules parameter recovery, posterior predictive checks, model comparison. Overall, facilitates study wide range cognitive processes tasks involving perceptual decision making, memory retrieval, value-based decision making.

title: Evidence Accumulation Models
text: Documentation (users): User-facing documentation tutorials, usage examples, model overviews. Development repository (developers): GitHub repository source code, development discussions, issue/PR tracking. main branch reflects development version, tags used stable releases. CRAN page: official CRAN page eam package.

title: Evidence Accumulation Models
text: Pure C++ core fast vectorization-friendly algorithms designed. 100x speedup compared naive python implementations. Memory-efficient computation, enabling 10 million trials standard desktop machine. Parallel execution near-linear speed-across multiple CPU cores. Flexible architecture: composable api, defines several formulas, can easily customize models. modern R api’╝ÜBuilt around modern R conventions, provides declarative, tidy-style interface. Unified data pipeline: provides consistent set data manipulation summarization utilities seamless simulation, evaluation, visualization.

title: Evidence Accumulation Models

title: Approximate Bayesian Computation wrapper ŌĆö abc_abc
code: # \donttest{ # Load example simulation output and observed data rdm_minimal_example <- system.file("extdata", "rdm_minimal", package = "eam") sim_output <- load_simulation_output(file.path(rdm_minimal_example, "simulation")) obs_df <- read.csv(file.path(rdm_minimal_example, "observation", "observation_data.csv"))  # Define a summary-statistics pipeline summary_pipe <- summarise_by(   .by = c("condition_idx"),   rt_mean = mean(rt) )  # Summarise simulation output and observed data sim_summary <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = FALSE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) {     summary_pipe(cond_df)   } ) obs_summary <- summary_pipe(obs_df)  # Build ABC input abc_input <- build_abc_input(   simulation_output = sim_output,   simulation_summary = sim_summary,   target_summary = obs_summary,   param = c("V_beta_1", "V_beta_group") )  # Fit an ABC model abc_rejection_model <- abc_abc(   abc_input = abc_input,   tol = 0.5,   method = "rejection" ) # }

title: Cross-validation for ABC model ŌĆö abc_cv
code: # \donttest{ # Load example simulation output and observed data rdm_minimal_example <- system.file("extdata", "rdm_minimal", package = "eam") sim_output <- load_simulation_output(file.path(rdm_minimal_example, "simulation")) obs_df <- read.csv(file.path(rdm_minimal_example, "observation", "observation_data.csv"))  # Define a summary-statistics pipeline summary_pipe <- summarise_by(   .by = c("condition_idx"),   rt_mean = mean(rt) )  # Summarise simulation output and observed data sim_summary <- map_by_condition(   sim_output,   .progress = FALSE,   .parallel = FALSE,   function(cond_df) {     summary_pipe(cond_df)   } ) obs_summary <- summary_pipe(obs_df)  # Build ABC input and fit an ABC model abc_input <- build_abc_input(   simulation_output = sim_output,   simulation_summary = sim_summary,   target_summary = obs_summary,   param = c("V_beta_1", "V_beta_group") ) abc_model <- abc_abc(   abc_input = abc_input,   tol = 0.5,   method = "rejection" )  # Run cross-validation for the fitted ABC model abc_cv_result <- abc_cv(   abc_input = abc_input,   abc_result = abc_model,   nval = 10,   tols = c(0.1, 0.5) ) # }

title: ABC posterior predictive check ŌĆö abc_posterior_predictive_check
text: invisible(NULL). function used plotting side   effects prints RT accuracy plots directly.

title: ABC posterior predictive check ŌĆö abc_posterior_predictive_check
text: function teaching quick demonstrations. intentionally specific one input shape (abc object). step checks, follow functions: abc_posterior_bootstrap(), update_config_from_posterior(), run_simulation(). wrapper mainly teaching tool. provides compact end--end posterior predictive workflow, intentionally hides several modeling choices collapsing posterior single summary simulating reduced representation. serious work, manual posterior predictive simulation preferred. recommended workflow draw posterior parameter values explicitly abc_posterior_bootstrap(), inspect modify draws needed, rebuild simulation configuration explicitly new_simulation_config parameter structure fully control, run simulation run_simulation(), compare simulated output observed data using plotting summary functions. update_config_from_posterior() can still useful quick checks, rebuilding config safer option need know exactly posterior values mapped back model. Following steps manually makes assumption visible, including posterior draw used, parameter values entered simulation config, posterior predictive data generated.

title: ABC posterior predictive check ŌĆö abc_posterior_predictive_check
code: # \donttest{ # Load example simulation config, fitted ABC model, and observed data base_dir <- system.file("extdata", "rdm_minimal", package = "eam") sim_output <- load_simulation_output(file.path(base_dir, "simulation")) abc_model <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "abc", "abc_neuralnet_model.rds")) obs_df <- read.csv(file.path(base_dir, "observation", "observation_data.csv"))  # Run a high-level posterior predictive check abc_posterior_predictive_check(   config = sim_output$simulation_config,   abc_result = abc_model,   observed_df = obs_df,   n_conditions = 1,   n_trials_per_condition = 500,   rt_facet_x = c("item_idx"),   rt_facet_y = c(),   accuracy_x = "item_idx",   accuracy_facet_x = c("group"),   accuracy_facet_y = c() ) #> Error in accumulate_evidence_ddm_2b(item_params$A_upper, item_params$A_lower,     item_params$V, Z, item_params$ndt, max_t, dt, max_reached,     noise_mechanism, noise_fun): could not find function "accumulate_evidence_ddm_2b" # }
